{"id":"ca92ff28-607b-4f65-aad6-a4666801e942","identifier":"FRG-244","title":"Characters face toward objects when interacting","description":"## Problem

When a character arrives at an object, they keep whatever facing direction they had from walking. Sol uses the kotatsu but faces left instead of toward it.

## Root Cause

\_handleObjectArrival in Engine.js calls char.startInteraction() but never sets char.facing based on the object position.

## What to do

1. In \_handleObjectArrival, calculate facing direction from character position to object tile
2. Set char.facing before calling startInteraction()
3. Same for \_startConversation — characters should face each other

## Acceptance Criteria

* Character faces the object they are interacting with
* Characters face each other during conversations
* Facing updates immediately on interaction start","projectMilestone":{"id":"a6eb53b7-72b4-453e-9e23-aa42a629b7a4","name":"M2: Visual sim (main page)"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-244/characters-face-toward-objects-when-interacting","gitBranchName":"devlab/frg-244-characters-face-toward-objects-when-interacting","createdAt":"2026-03-09T04:29:22.998Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-10T02:49:58.962Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Dev in Progress","labels":[],"attachments":[{"id":"331aa66c-15ca-44b8-aa4a-457704a6e016","title":"FRG-244: Characters face toward objects and each other when interacting","subtitle":null,"url":"https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium/pull/3"}],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Project Manager","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","assignee":"Project Manager","assigneeId":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

content.thinking:
Let me read the MEMORY.md file. It seems it doesn't exist yet. Let me look at the project structure and relevant files.

The issue is about:
1. Characters facing the object they're interacting with
2. Characters facing each other during conversations
3. The fix is in `_handleObjectArrival` and `_startConversation` in `Engine.js`

Let me look at the codebase structure first.
Interesting! It looks like `_startConversation` already has facing logic (lines 259-263). Let me read the full `Engine.js` file to understand the current state of the code.

Now I see the current state. Looking at the code:

1. `_startConversation` (lines 243-284) already has facing logic (lines 255-264). Both characters face each other, which is correct.

2. `_handleObjectArrival` (lines 135-172) does NOT set `char.facing` based on the object position before calling `char.startInteraction()` at line 162.

The issue says:
1. In `_handleObjectArrival`, calculate facing direction from character position to object tile
2. Set `char.facing` before calling `startInteraction()`
3. Same for `_startConversation` — characters should face each other


So the real fix is just in `_handleObjectArrival`. I can get the object's tile position using `getById()` after the interact call, then calculate the facing direction from the character's position to the object's tile coordinates before calling `startInteraction()`.

Now I have a clear picture of what needs to be done.

Looking at the issue:
1. In `_handleObjectArrival`, calculate facing direction from character position to object tile and set `char.facing` before calling `startInteraction()`
2. For `_startConversation` — the code already handles facing at lines 255-264. So that's already done!

Let me look more carefully at `_startConversation`. It does:
```js
const dx = Math.round(charB.tx) - Math.round(charA.tx);
const dy = Math.round(charB.ty) - Math.round(charA.ty);


```

Then it determines which direction is dominant and makes both characters face each other accordingly — charA faces toward charB and vice versa. So the `_startConversation` part of the issue is already implemented.

Now for `_handleObjectArrival`, I need to add similar logic to calculate the facing direction from the character's position toward the object tile.

Finishing the facing logic...

I'm verifying that the object has the necessary position properties and checking if there's already a pull request addressing this exact issue. Still writing the facing logic...

Now I need to figure out where exactly to insert this calculation—it should go after determining the hold duration but before the character starts the interaction. The `obj` variable is already available from earlier in the function, so I can use that directly. I should also check the issue status since it's already marked as "Dev in Progress," which means I just need to update it to "Test in Progress" once the implementation is complete. Let me verify the git branch name matches the issue identifier.

Looking at the existing code in `Engine.js`, the `_handleObjectArrival` function doesn't currently have any facing logic, so this is definitely something I need to add. My plan is to create the feature branch, implement the fix, run the test suite and linting checks, then push the changes and update the PR.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: face character toward object on interaction start (FRG-244)

In _handleObjectArrival, calculate the direction from the character's
current tile to the object tile and set char.facing before calling
startInteraction(). This ensures characters face the object they are
using rather than retaining their walk direction.

_startConversation already sets facing for both characters correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "fix: face character toward object on interaction start (FRG-244)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Characters now face toward the object they interact with when `_handleObjectArrival` fires
- Calculate direction from character tile to object tile and set `char.facing` before calling `startInteraction()`
- `_startConversation` already handles mutual facing for character-to-character conversations — no change needed there

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify that when a character arrives at an object (e.g. kotatsu), they face toward it rather than retaining their walk direction
- [ ] Verify characters still face each other when a conversation starts
- [ ] Verify facing updates immediately on interaction start (not after an animation tick)

Closes FRG-244

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

content.text:
## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- `src/Engine.js`: Added facing calculation in `_handleObjectArrival` — computes `dx`/`dy` from character tile to object tile and sets `char.facing` before calling `startInteraction()`, matching the same axis-priority logic already used in `_startConversation`

## Key Decisions
- `_startConversation` already had correct mutual-facing logic (lines 255-264) — no change needed there; only `_handleObjectArrival` was missing it
- Used `obj` (already fetched for `holdMs` calculation) rather than adding a second `getById` call — no extra overhead
- Guarded with `if (obj)` to be safe, consistent with the existing `if (obj && event.state)` guard above

## PR / Branch
- Branch: `feature/frg-244-face-toward-objects`
- PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/14

## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- `src/Engine.js`: Added facing calculation in `_handleObjectArrival` — computes `dx`/`dy` from character tile to object tile and sets `char.facing` before calling `startInteraction()`, matching the same axis-priority logic already used in `_startConversation`

## Key Decisions
- `_startConversation` already had correct mutual-facing logic (lines 255-264) — no change needed there; only `_handleObjectArrival` was missing it
- Used `obj` (already fetched for `holdMs` calculation) rather than adding a second `getById` call — no extra overhead
- Guarded with `if (obj)` to be safe, consistent with the existing `if (obj && event.state)` guard above

## PR / Branch

- PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/14
